import math
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class ConvBlock(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, in_ch, out_ch, k=3, p=1, dropout=0.0):
        super().__init__()
        self.net = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(in_ch, out_ch, k, padding=p, bias=False),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(out_ch),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Conv2d(out_ch, out_ch, k, padding=p, bias=False),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(out_ch),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout2d(dropout) if dropout > 0 else nn.Identity(),
        )
        self.proj = nn.Conv2d(in_ch, out_ch, 1, bias=False) if in_ch != out_ch else nn.Identity()

    def forward(self, x):
        return self.net(x) + self.proj(x)


class Encoder(nn.Module):
    """CNN that aggressively reduces height but keeps reasonable horizontal resolution."""
    def __init__(self, enc_dim=256):
        super().__init__()
        self.stem = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Conv2d(1, 32, 3, padding=1, bias=False),
            nn.BatchNorm2d(32),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
        )
        self.b1 = ConvBlock(32, 64, dropout=0.02)
        self.b2 = ConvBlock(64, 128, dropout=0.03)
        self.b3 = ConvBlock(128, 192, dropout=0.04)
        self.b4 = ConvBlock(192, enc_dim, dropout=0.05)
        self.rnn = nn.LSTM(enc_dim, enc_dim // 2, num_layers=1, batch_first=True, bidirectional=True)

    def forward(self, x):
        # x: B 1 H W; mask valid pixels unused, images are padded white/zero after inversion
        x = self.stem(x)
        x = self.b1(x)
        x = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=(2, 2), stride=(2, 2))      # H/2 W/2
        x = self.b2(x)
        x = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=(2, 1), stride=(2, 1))      # H/4 W/2
        x = self.b3(x)
        x = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=(2, 2), stride=(2, 2))      # H/8 W/4
        x = self.b4(x)
        x = F.max_pool2d(x, kernel_size=(2, 1), stride=(2, 1))      # H/16 W/4
        x = x.mean(dim=2).transpose(1, 2).contiguous()             # B T C
        x, _ = self.rnn(x)
        return x


class AttentionDecoder(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, enc_dim=256, emb_dim=128, dec_dim=256, dropout=0.2, pad_id=0):
        super().__init__()
        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
        self.pad_id = pad_id
        self.emb = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, emb_dim, padding_idx=pad_id)
        self.init_h = nn.Linear(enc_dim, dec_dim)
        self.init_c = nn.Linear(enc_dim, dec_dim)
        self.attn_enc = nn.Linear(enc_dim, dec_dim, bias=False)
        self.attn_dec = nn.Linear(dec_dim, dec_dim, bias=False)
        self.attn_v = nn.Linear(dec_dim, 1, bias=False)
        self.lstm = nn.LSTMCell(emb_dim + enc_dim, dec_dim)
        self.out = nn.Sequential(
            nn.Linear(dec_dim + enc_dim + emb_dim, dec_dim),
            nn.ReLU(inplace=True),
            nn.Dropout(dropout),
            nn.Linear(dec_dim, vocab_size),
        )
        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)

    def attend(self, enc, enc_proj, h):
        e = self.attn_v(torch.tanh(enc_proj + self.attn_dec(h).unsqueeze(1))).squeeze(-1)
        a = torch.softmax(e, dim=1)
        ctx = torch.bmm(a.unsqueeze(1), enc).squeeze(1)
        return ctx, a

    def forward(self, enc, tgt_in):
        # tgt_in: B L, starts with sos. returns logits B L V
        pooled = enc.mean(dim=1)
        h = torch.tanh(self.init_h(pooled))
        c = torch.tanh(self.init_c(pooled))
        enc_proj = self.attn_enc(enc)
        ctx = torch.zeros(enc.size(0), enc.size(2), device=enc.device, dtype=enc.dtype)
        outs = []
        emb = self.dropout(self.emb(tgt_in))
        for t in range(tgt_in.size(1)):
            inp = torch.cat([emb[:, t], ctx], dim=-1)
            h, c = self.lstm(inp, (h, c))
            ctx, _ = self.attend(enc, enc_proj, h)
            outs.append(self.out(torch.cat([h, ctx, emb[:, t]], dim=-1)))
        return torch.stack(outs, dim=1)

    @torch.no_grad()
    def greedy(self, enc, sos_id=1, eos_id=2, max_len=160):
        b = enc.size(0)
        pooled = enc.mean(dim=1)
        h = torch.tanh(self.init_h(pooled))
        c = torch.tanh(self.init_c(pooled))
        enc_proj = self.attn_enc(enc)
        ctx = torch.zeros(b, enc.size(2), device=enc.device, dtype=enc.dtype)
        tok = torch.full((b,), sos_id, dtype=torch.long, device=enc.device)
        seqs = [[] for _ in range(b)]
        done = torch.zeros(b, dtype=torch.bool, device=enc.device)
        for _ in range(max_len):
            emb = self.emb(tok)
            h, c = self.lstm(torch.cat([emb, ctx], dim=-1), (h, c))
            ctx, _ = self.attend(enc, enc_proj, h)
            logits = self.out(torch.cat([h, ctx, emb], dim=-1))
            tok = logits.argmax(dim=-1)
            for i in range(b):
                if not done[i]:
                    v = int(tok[i].item())
                    if v == eos_id:
                        done[i] = True
                    else:
                        seqs[i].append(v)
            if bool(done.all()):
                break
        return seqs


class HMERModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size, enc_dim=256, emb_dim=128, dec_dim=256, dropout=0.2, pad_id=0):
        super().__init__()
        self.encoder = Encoder(enc_dim=enc_dim)
        self.decoder = AttentionDecoder(vocab_size, enc_dim, emb_dim, dec_dim, dropout, pad_id)

    def forward(self, images, tgt_in):
        enc = self.encoder(images)
        return self.decoder(enc, tgt_in)

    @torch.no_grad()
    def greedy(self, images, sos_id=1, eos_id=2, max_len=160):
        enc = self.encoder(images)
        return self.decoder.greedy(enc, sos_id=sos_id, eos_id=eos_id, max_len=max_len)
